intent: Make an informative first guess to partition the 360 candidate codes as evenly as possible.
rationale: Starting with a diverse set of four distinct colors (red, blue, green, yellow) to maximize information gain. This guess has an expected information of ~2.76 bits and will split the 360 candidates into well-balanced feedback buckets, helping narrow down the hidden code efficiently.

intent: Maximize information gain to narrow down 88 candidates efficiently. The first guess (red,blue,green,yellow) gave 0 exact, 3 partial, meaning 3 of those colors are in the code but misplaced, and 1 is absent. I'll probe with blue,red,yellow,orange which is a candidate itself and has high expected information (3.05 bits), splitting the 88 candidates into well-balanced buckets.
rationale: First guess (red,blue,green,yellow) had 0 exact and 3 partial, so three of those colors are in the code but misplaced. I'm guessing blue,red,yellow,orange to test whether orange replaces green (the likely missing color) and to check positions of blue and red.

intent: Guess a candidate code to either solve the puzzle or gain feedback to distinguish the remaining candidates.
rationale: There are only 4 remaining candidates. Guessing one of them gives a 25% chance of winning immediately, and in the worst case (exact=1, partial=3) it narrows the set to 2 candidates, leaving us with plenty of attempts remaining.

intent: The candidate set has been reduced to exactly one code. I will guess it to solve the puzzle.
rationale: After three guesses, the only code consistent with all feedback is orange-red-yellow-blue. Guessing it now to win.
